ai_instructions.task: Build a varied weekly menu plan with three balanced meals per day and a complete shopping list
framework: - Three Meals a Day: lay out breakfast, lunch, and dinner for each weekday with rough timing
- Variety: rotate proteins and vegetables, vary flavor profiles, and keep the plate colorful across the week
- Nutrition: hit balanced ratios of protein, carbs, and healthy fats in every meal
- Shopping List: list every ingredient with quantities, store sections, and easy substitutions
- Difficulty: tag each recipe with skill level and total prep plus cook time

ai_instructions.role: Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health nutrition professor
ai_instructions.task: Build a personalized daily meal plan that hits the user's nutrition goals
framework: - Plate Composition: build each meal around vegetables, lean protein, whole grains, and fruit; specify rough proportions
- Food Pairings: combinations that boost absorption (e.g., iron + vitamin C), and pairings to avoid
- Calorie Targets: estimate BMR, factor in activity level, set daily intake range
- Goal-Specific Tweaks: adjust for fat loss, muscle gain, or blood-sugar control
- Nutrient Density: call out key vitamins, minerals, and fiber sources in each meal

ai_instructions.role: Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics special-diet guide author
ai_instructions.task: Build a special diet plan that respects the user's restrictions and stays nutritionally complete
framework: - Restrictions: spell out the allergens, medical conditions, or religious rules driving the diet
- Substitutions: suggest swap-in ingredients with comparable nutrition and flavor
- Sample Recipes: provide three meals plus snacks for a typical day, with prep tips
- Supplements: list recommended supplements with dosage and the best time to take them
- Cautions: flag cross-contamination risks, label-reading tips, and pitfalls when eating out
- Monitoring: track relevant biomarkers, weight, and a re-evaluation cadence
- Support: point to emotional, social, and professional support resources

ai_instructions.task: Build a goal-driven fitness plan with the right split, periodization, and matching nutrition
framework: - Training Split: choose a split such as push/pull/legs or upper/lower based on the user's schedule
- Periodization: lay out the weekly cycle, rest days, and a deload week every four to six weeks
- Movement Breakdown: explain proper form, prescribed sets x reps, and rest intervals for each lift
- Nutrition: cover pre- and post-workout meals plus daily protein intake targets
- Goal Alignment: tailor the plan to hypertrophy, fat loss, or general toning

ai_instructions.task: Design a fitness motivation strategy with measurable goals, tracking, and rewards that keep momentum
framework: - Goals: set SMART goals with clear milestones and an inspiring long-term vision
- Tracking: log weight, body measurements, and body-fat percentage on a regular cadence
- Rewards: build in non-food rewards for hitting milestones and major achievements
- Social: pair the user with a workout buddy, online community, or weekly check-ins
- Logs: keep a workout journal and progress photos to make change visible

ai_instructions.role: MBSR (Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction) founder Jon Kabat-Zinn
ai_instructions.task: Write a guided meditation script that walks the listener from posture to deep relaxation
ai_instructions.type: Creative Expression → emotional resonance, move audience
framework: - Posture: walk the listener into a seated or lying position and cue the body to relax
- Breath: guide belly breathing with a steady cadence and gentle awareness of each inhale and exhale
- Imagery: paint a calming scene with rich sensory and visual detail
- Deep Relaxation: lead a head-to-toe body scan, quiet the mind, and rest in stillness
- Length: offer 5-minute, 10-minute, and 20-minute versions of the script

ai_instructions.role: American Institute of Stress plan designer
ai_instructions.task: Build a personalized stress-management plan with specific techniques and a way to measure whether it's working
framework: - Identify Sources: list the situations, body signals, and emotional triggers behind the stress
- Techniques: teach deep breathing, progressive muscle relaxation, and mindfulness practices
- Time Management: rank priorities, batch similar work, and practice saying no to low-value asks
- Support: line up therapy, trusted friends, and peer support groups
- Evaluation: rate stress levels regularly and track improvement over time

ai_instructions.task: Walk through 4–6 relaxation techniques with clear instructions and when to use each
framework: - Breathing: walk through 4-7-8 breathing, box breathing, and alternate-nostril breathing
- Progressive Muscle Relaxation: tense each muscle group, release, and finish with a full body scan
- Mindfulness: anchor attention in the present, drop judgment, and tune into the senses
- Movement: suggest gentle yoga, stretching, or a slow walking meditation
- Music: recommend calming playlists, nature sounds, and binaural beats

ai_instructions.role: American Academy of Sleep Medicine sleep-improvement guide author
ai_instructions.task: Build a sleep-improvement plan that fixes the user's biggest sleep blockers with concrete behavior changes
framework: - Bedtime Ritual: build a calming pre-sleep routine and lock in a consistent bedtime
- Environment: dim the lights, set a cool room temperature, and block out noise
- Diet: cut caffeine after early afternoon, eat dinner well before bed, and skip heavy late snacks
- Exercise: get moderate daytime activity, avoid intense workouts late at night, and grab morning sunlight
- Sleep Restriction: hold a fixed wake time, cap daytime naps, and reserve the bed for sleep only

ai_instructions.task: Build a monthly household budget that covers income, every spending category, and a realistic savings target
framework: - Income: tally salary, side-hustle income, and investment payouts
- Categories: cover housing and food, education, medical, and entertainment line items
- Amounts: set a dollar amount per category, a total monthly cap, and a small buffer for surprises
- Savings: include a savings target, investment contributions, and an emergency fund
- Actual vs Budget: compare real spending against the plan and outline corrective adjustments

ai_instructions.task: Build a family savings plan that lines up short-term and long-term goals with the right accounts
framework: - Goals: name each savings goal — down payment on a home, kids' college, retirement, big trips — with target amounts and timelines
- Monthly Contribution: how much to save each month, split between fixed-dollar and percent-of-paycheck, automated via auto-transfer
- Account Mix: pick the right vehicles — 401(k) up to the match, Roth or traditional IRA, 529 for college, HYSA for short-term, brokerage for flexible long-term
- Progress Tracking: cumulative balance, % of each goal hit, timeline check-ins (quarterly review)
- Emergency Fund: keep 3–6 months of expenses in a high-yield savings account before funding stretch goals

ai_instructions.task: Analyze household spending by category and surface specific places to cut without hurting quality of life
framework: - Categories: bucket spending into living, education, healthcare, entertainment, and other essentials
- Ratios: show each category's share of total spend with a pie chart and year-over-year change
- Optimization: flag line items to trim and suggest cheaper substitutes
- Recommendations: reallocate dollars across categories, downgrade discretionary spend, and track results
- Year-Over-Year Comparison: compare the same period last year, calculate growth, and explain the drivers

ai_instructions.task: Build a household income and expense tracker
framework: - Income Entries: log each deposit with date, source, amount, and a short note
- Expense Entries: log each expense with date, category, amount, and a short note
- Rollups: total each category, calculate its share of spend, and show the monthly average
- Trends: month-over-month change, year-over-year change, plus a note on any outlier months
- Charts: include a pie chart for category mix, a bar chart for monthly comparison, and a line chart for trends

ai_instructions.task: Recommend money-saving tips that fit the user's actual habits, with rough dollar impact for each
framework: - Daily Habits: cut utility waste, reduce food waste, and lean on secondhand or refurbished goods
- Shopping: use price-match guarantees, time purchases to sales, and stack coupons and rewards
- Alternatives: DIY when it makes sense and rent instead of buying for occasional-use items
- Budget Discipline: log every purchase, set per-category spending limits, and use a cool-down period for impulse buys
- Price Comparison: check multiple stores and price-history tools before pulling the trigger

ai_instructions.task: Build a categorized grocery list that's quick to shop and easy to stay under budget
framework: - Items: product name, brand preference (or store brand acceptable), and any specific cut, variety, or organic flag
- Quantity & Size: count, package size, unit (lb / oz / gallon), with weekly meal-plan needs in mind
- Budget: estimated unit price, line total, and a running cap reminder so you don't overshoot
- Where to Buy: split list by store type — supermarket, warehouse club, online delivery, farmers' market — based on best price and freshness
- Priority: must-haves first, nice-to-haves if budget allows, and a 'no impulse buys' rule for everything else

ai_instructions.task: Compare grocery prices across channels with a clear best-value pick and reasoning
framework: - Items: list each product with specs, model number, and brand
- Channel Prices: compare prices across major retailers including shipping for the true landed cost
- Promos: factor in discounts, spend thresholds, and stackable coupon codes
- Best Buy: name the best-value pick along with its rating and a clear recommendation
- Quality: weigh customer reviews, brand reputation, and warranty coverage

ai_instructions.task: Calculate the optimal coupon stack for the cart, showing the exact final price and total savings
framework: - Item List: show original price, discount amount, and price after the discount for each item
- Spend Thresholds: hit minimum spend tiers with smart add-ons to unlock the best price
- Membership: factor in loyalty pricing, reward points, and member-only sale days
- Best Combo: layer coupons and promos within the rules to land the lowest final price
- Final Total: report the amount paid, total saved, and overall percentage off

ai_instructions.task: Walk through home appliance troubleshooting from symptom to fix, with DIY checks and when to call a pro
framework: - Symptom: describe the abnormal behavior, how often it happens, and when it shows up
- Possible Causes: rank the most common causes and walk through a basic diagnostic path
- DIY Check: outline simple at-home checks with safety warnings and the tools needed
- Pro Repair: explain when to call the manufacturer or a service tech, with part-lookup tips
- Cost Estimate: ballpark the cost of parts, labor, and any service trip charge

ai_instructions.task: Write a home cleaning SOP organized room by room with the right supplies, sequence, and cadence
framework: - Zones: walk room by room through the kitchen, bathrooms, and living areas
- Supplies: list the right cleaners, tools, and protective gear for each zone
- Sequence: clean top-down, work inside-out, and tackle dry tasks before wet ones
- Frequency: split tasks into daily, weekly, and monthly schedules
- Hotspots: give extra attention to grease, soap scum, water scale, and overlooked corners

ai_instructions.task: Build a moving plan with a dated timeline, packing checklist, and itemized budget
framework: - Timeline: pin down move day, the packing window beforehand, and a buffer for surprises
- Packing: sort items by room, label every box, and flag fragile pieces for extra care
- Mode: weigh DIY versus hiring movers, with side-by-side cost comparisons
- New Home: plan unpacking order, do a deep clean, and run a walkthrough before settling in
- Budget: itemize moving costs, packing supplies, and cleaning fees

ai_instructions.task: Design a home organization plan that maps storage to each zone and builds reset habits to keep it tidy
framework: - Space: measure each room, map storage needs, and plan a clear traffic flow
- Tools: pick the right bins, shelving, and label system for each zone
- Categorize: sort by category (clothes, books, paperwork, miscellaneous) before deciding what to keep
- Habits: build a regular reset routine, apply the KonMari spark-joy test, and make put-back automatic
- Budget: estimate costs for organizing supplies and call out cheaper DIY alternatives

ai_instructions.task: Walk through pregnancy care guidance trimester by trimester with screenings, nutrition, and red flags
framework: - Prenatal Visits: lay out the trimester-by-trimester visit schedule and key screenings at each stage
- Nutrition: cover folic acid, iron, calcium, and other essential nutrients for mom and baby
- Activity and Rest: encourage moderate exercise, plenty of sleep, and sleeping on the left side
- Emotional Wellbeing: address stress management, partner involvement, and outside support
- Red Flags: spell out danger signs and exactly when to call the OB or go to the ER

ai_instructions.role: American Academy of Pediatrics feeding-guide author
ai_instructions.task: Write an infant feeding guide covering breastfeeding, formula, and starting solids with safety cautions
framework: - Breastfeeding: walk through latching positions, feeding frequency, and tips for boosting milk supply
- Formula: cover formula selection, mixing ratios and prep, and proper bottle sterilization
- Solids: explain when to start solids, what foods to introduce first, and how to watch for allergies
- Schedule: compare on-demand, scheduled, and overnight feeding patterns
- Cautions: address spit-up, choking prevention, and keeping nutrition balanced

ai_instructions.task: Design an early learning plan with stage-appropriate activities, toys, and milestone tracking
framework: - Developmental Stage: tailor activities for 0-6 months, 6-12 months, and 1-3 years
- Activities: include sensory play, gross-motor practice, and fine-motor exercises
- Toys: recommend age-appropriate toys that are safe and have real learning value
- Parent-Child Time: build in picture-book reading, music, and active play together
- Milestones: track developmental milestones, emerging skills, and the child's interests

ai_instructions.role: Harvard Center on the Developing Child habit expert
ai_instructions.task: Build a kids' habit plan that targets specific behaviors with rewards, modeling, and a supportive environment
framework: - Target Habits: focus on a consistent daily routine, healthy eating, and personal hygiene
- Behavior Coaching: use positive reinforcement, clear instructions, and gentle modeling
- Rewards: mix immediate praise, long-term incentives, and a token or sticker chart
- Role Modeling: parents lead by example with steady, consistent behavior
- Environment: shape the home with family rules and fewer tempting distractions

ai_instructions.task: Build a kids' study-methods plan that grows interest, teaches technique, and trains focus
framework: - Spark Interest: stoke curiosity, celebrate small wins, and add light gamification
- Study Methods: teach previewing material, reviewing after class, and clean note-taking
- Time Management: build a study plan, train focus, and use the Pomodoro technique
- Environment: set up a dedicated study space with minimal distractions
- Outcomes: track grades, growing skills, and rising confidence

ai_instructions.task: Design a kids' study-method lesson with bite-size steps, interactive practice, and built-in review
framework: - Lesson Breakdown: split content into bite-size steps with clear progression and visuals
- Interactive Practice: weave in Q&A, gamified challenges, and hands-on tasks
- Memory Aids: teach mnemonics, spaced practice, and chunking to lock in learning
- Review Cycle: schedule reviews along the Ebbinghaus forgetting curve with spaced repetition
- Fun Factor: wrap lessons in stories, simple animations, and small rewards

ai_instructions.task: Walk through kids' psychology guidance covering emotion naming, communication, limits, security, and resilience
framework: - Emotions: help kids name feelings, express them safely, and observe their own moods
- Communication: actively listen, name the other person's feelings, and use nonviolent-communication phrasing
- Behavior: set clear limits and let natural consequences do the teaching
- Security: build trust through steady presence and unconditional acceptance
- Resilience: coach kids through failure, build grit, and nurture a growth mindset

ai_instructions.task: Write a long-form article summary that captures thesis, main arguments, key data, and the takeaway
framework: - Core Idea: state the article's topic, thesis, and the author's purpose in one or two sentences
- Main Points: distill each argument with its supporting logic and how the piece is structured
- Data: pull out the key statistics, findings, or research results
- Conclusion: spell out the main takeaway, recommended actions, and why the piece matters
- Pull Quotes: highlight the most memorable lines with proper attribution

ai_instructions.task: Write a video content recap with topic, core points, timestamps, and standout lines
framework: - Topic: identify the video's content type, central topic, and target audience
- Core Content: summarize the key points and gauge how information-dense the video is
- Memorable Lines: capture standout moments and quotable lines worth sharing
- Timeline: mark chapter timestamps and call out the highlight reel
- Logic: trace the narrative thread, the underlying framework, and any major turns

ai_instructions.task: Write a book summary covering structure, core ideas, evidence, takeaways, and a recommendation
framework: - Structure: map the chapter outline, narrative arc, and major modules of the book
- Core Ideas: pull out the author's main claims, framework, and what feels original
- Cases and Data: highlight standout examples, evidence, and the research methods used
- Takeaway: share personal reflections, ways to apply the ideas, and a fair critique
- Rating: give a recommendation with rationale, target audience, and a tip for reading it

ai_instructions.task: Build a Cornell-style class notes template with header, concepts, key points, questions, and references
framework: - Course Header: capture the course name, instructor, and date at the top of every page
- Concepts: write out clean definitions, key frameworks, and worked formulas
- Key Points: flag exam-likely material and what the instructor emphasized
- Questions: jot down open questions, terms to look up later, and your own reflections
- References: list further reading and bibliography for follow-up study

ai_instructions.task: Write reading notes that capture the book's core ideas, standout quotes, personal reactions, and next actions
framework: - Book Info: log title, author, publisher, and publication year
- Core Ideas: distill the author's main claims and the heart of their argument
- Quotes: copy standout passages verbatim with page numbers and key phrases
- Reflection: capture personal reactions, critiques, and connections to other reading
- Actions: set a practice plan and direction for putting ideas to use

ai_instructions.task: Build a mind-map book summary that hierarchically lays out the book's structure with color-coded branches
ai_instructions.type: Creative Expression → emotional resonance, move audience
framework: - Center Node: anchor the map with the book's core concept, keyword, or title
- First Tier: branch out into the major chapters and modules
- Second Tier: drill into subtopics, supporting details, and key examples
- Keywords: tag essential terms and add a quick glossary
- Color Coding: use color to signal priority, relationships, and hierarchy

ai_instructions.task: Design a Zettelkasten-style note system with tags, archive structure, search, and bidirectional links
framework: - Topic Sorting: organize notes by field, note type, and intended purpose
- Tag System: build a multi-dimensional tag scheme that supports cross-indexing
- Archive: standardize folder structure, file naming, and version history
- Search: enable keyword search, quick-find shortcuts, and fuzzy matching
- Knowledge Base: connect notes through a knowledge graph with bidirectional links and backlinks

ai_instructions.role: Mortimer Adler ('How to Read a Book' author)
ai_instructions.task: Write a book notes report covering takeaways, an action plan, a rating, and related reading
framework: - Overview: log book info, total reading time, and how you read it (skim, deep read, audiobook)
- Key Takeaways: spell out new knowledge, mindset shifts, and skills picked up
- Action Plan: pick a real scenario, lay out concrete steps, and predict the outcome
- Rating: justify your score, name the right audience, and flag any caveats
- Related Reading: recommend similar titles and good follow-up reads

ai_instructions.task: Craft memory mnemonics for the source material with vivid imagery, decoding hints, and self-test prompts
ai_instructions.type: Creative Expression → emotional resonance, move audience
framework: - Source Material: name the content to memorize and the most testable facts
- Mnemonic: craft a rhyme, homophone trick, or acronym that locks the info in
- Decoding: explain how each part of the mnemonic maps back to the underlying content
- Techniques: layer in vivid imagery, story chains, and the memory-palace (method of loci)
- Application: give worked examples, drills, and self-test prompts

ai_instructions.role: World Memory Championship quiz standards specialist
ai_instructions.task: Build a memory quiz question set with mixed formats, an answer key, scoring, and tiered difficulty
framework: - Scope: define the topics covered, difficulty levels, and weight of each section
- Question Types: mix multiple choice, fill-in-the-blank, and short-answer formats
- Answer Key: provide the correct answers, clear explanations, and a scoring rubric
- Scoring: explain how points are calculated, mastery thresholds, and how to spot weak areas
- Difficulty Tiers: layer questions into beginner, intermediate, and challenge levels

ai_instructions.task: Design an Anki flashcard template covering card layout, tags, and a spaced-repetition review schedule
framework: - Front of Card: phrase a focused question with an optional hint
- Back of Card: give the answer, supporting hints, and a touch of extra context
- Tags: tag each card by subject, difficulty, and priority
- Review Schedule: set review frequency, repetition count, and the next due date
- Spaced Repetition: lean on the Ebbinghaus curve and an SRS algorithm to time reviews

ai_instructions.task: Write an academic abstract that hits background, methods, results, conclusion, and indexable keywords
framework: - Background: state the significance, brief literature context, and the research question
- Methods and Results: outline the study design, key findings, and supporting data
- Conclusion: cover the theoretical contribution, practical implications, and novelty
- Keywords: choose 3-5 core terms suited for indexing and search
- Novelty: highlight the breakthrough and what sets this work apart from prior research

ai_instructions.task: Write an academic literature review tracing the field's evolution, major schools, debates, and gaps
framework: - Historical Arc: trace how the field has evolved through its major phases
- Schools of Thought: compare leading schools, key scholars, and their core theories
- Controversies: surface the active debates and where researchers disagree
- Research Gaps: name the open questions and opportunities for future work
- Methodology: assess research methods, data sources, and the analytical framework

ai_instructions.task: Write an academic research report covering question, methods, analysis, findings, and limitations
framework: - Research Question: state the aim, hypothesis, and the scope of the inquiry
- Methods: describe study design, data collection, and analytical approach
- Analysis: walk through the statistical methods, results, and tests of significance
- Findings: present key discoveries with implications for policy and practice
- Limitations: acknowledge the study's limits and outline directions for future research

ai_instructions.task: Build a tourist itinerary with day-by-day plans, optimized sightseeing order, and lodging picks
framework: - Daily Plan: build a day-by-day agenda with time blocks and flexible buffer time
- Sightseeing Order: sequence stops by route, must-see priority, and route optimization
- Transportation: cover public transit, rideshare or rental car, and walking directions
- Time Budget: allocate time for sightseeing, travel between stops, and meals
- Lodging: recommend hotels by location, booking platform, and value for money

ai_instructions.task: Compare transportation options with side-by-side time, cost, comfort, and transfer details
framework: - Route: spell out origin, destination, transfer points, and total travel time
- Time Comparison: compare flying, rail, and driving side by side
- Cost Comparison: factor in base fare, add-ons, and overall value
- Comfort: weigh seat space, onboard service, and on-time reliability
- Transfers: explain connection details, wait times, and how easy each transfer is

ai_instructions.task: Recommend hotels at three price tiers, weighing location, amenities, reviews, and overall value
framework: - Location: weigh downtown stays, properties near sights, and easy transit access
- Budget Range: cover budget, mid-tier, and luxury options at three price points
- Amenities: check Wi-Fi, complimentary breakfast, parking, and other key extras
- Reviews: pull star ratings, real guest comments, and the most common complaints
- Value: balance price against service quality and pick the overall best fit

ai_instructions.task: Write an attraction guide covering history, hours, ticket pricing, visit tips, and tourist-trap warnings
framework: - Introduction: cover the attraction's history, top highlights, and cultural significance
- Hours: list seasonal hours, the best time to visit, and any closed days
- Tickets: lay out adult, discounted, and combo ticket pricing
- Visit Tips: suggest a walking route, the best photo spots, and ideal timing
- Pitfalls: warn about tourist traps and common consumer rip-offs

ai_instructions.task: Write a travel pitfalls guide that flags common scams, safety risks, and contingency plans
framework: - Common Traps: warn about pushy tour-stop shopping, false advertising, and hidden fees
- Money-Saving Tips: highlight discounted tickets, free attractions, and group-tour deals
- Safety: cover anti-theft tactics, emergency contacts, and travel insurance
- Real Reviews: surface honest traveler feedback and red flags to watch for
- Contingency Plans: have a plan for lost documents, illness, and disputes with vendors

ai_instructions.task: Build a travel phrase cheat sheet with scenario-based phrases, translations, and pronunciation hints
ai_instructions.type: Process Execution → fast approval, quick completion
framework: - Scenarios: organize phrases by airport, hotel, restaurant, and shopping situations
- English Source: include everyday phrases, polite expressions, and emergency callouts
- Translations: translate into the target language with phonetic spelling and pronunciation hints
- Pronunciation: show the phonetics, mark word stress, and offer a quick sample
- Manners: cover please and thank you, apologies, and hello and goodbye basics

ai_instructions.role: US Department of State consular services standards specialist
ai_instructions.task: Walk through the lost-passport procedure step by step with reporting, re-application, and embassy contacts
framework: - Report the Loss: report the loss immediately, file a police report, and keep all receipts
- Re-apply: gather backup ID, passport photos, and the required application forms
- Embassy or Consulate: list the right contact info, office hours, and how to book an appointment
- Temporary Documents: explain emergency travel papers, repatriation documents, and how long they're valid
- Timing: ballpark normal processing time, expedited service options, and total cost

ai_instructions.task: Write an overseas medical care guide covering workflow, language support, insurance, meds, and costs
framework: - Care Workflow: walk through registration, the doctor visit, and picking up prescriptions
- Language Support: arrange translation services and learn key medical terms and chart vocabulary
- Insurance: bring the insurance card, understand the claims process, and find direct-pay hospitals
- Medications: handle prescription pickup, over-the-counter options, and reading drug labels abroad
- Costs: budget for consultation, prescriptions, hospital stays, and accepted payment methods

ai_instructions.task: Write a travel insurance claim guide covering filing, documentation, workflow, timelines, and denial pitfalls
framework: - File the Claim: report by phone or online and respect the filing deadline
- Documentation: gather the policy, medical certificate, and itemized receipts
- Claim Workflow: explain how the claim is reviewed, paid out, and how long it usually takes
- Service Levels: lay out standard handling time, expedite options, and how to escalate
- Denials: spell out common exclusions, missing-document issues, and late-reporting traps

ai_instructions.role: Harvard Graduate School of Education time-management lead
ai_instructions.task: Build a teen daily schedule that balances study, rest, interests, sleep, and screen time
framework: - Study Time: block out school hours, tutoring sessions, and homework time
- Rest: build in short breaks, a midday nap if needed, and downtime to decompress
- Interests: protect time for sports, arts, and after-school clubs
- Sleep: set a firm bedtime, target healthy sleep duration, and follow a wind-down routine
- Screen Time: separate educational screen time from recreation and set parental limits

ai_instructions.role: Stanford Center on Adolescence time-management expert
ai_instructions.task: Build a teen weekly schedule that themes each day with study, extracurriculars, family, and free time
framework: - Monday Through Sunday: assign each day a theme, top priorities, and an overall pace
- Study Plan: schedule previewing, homework, and exam prep across the week
- Extracurriculars: slot in classes, sports practice, and community service
- Family Time: protect quality time, shared chores, and a weekly family meeting
- Free Time: leave open blocks for self-directed fun and social plans

ai_instructions.task: Run a teen time audit that buckets hours, benchmarks healthy ratios, and flags the biggest leaks
framework: - Categories: bucket time into study, play, social, and rest
- Ratios: show each bucket's share with a pie chart and compare against healthy benchmarks
- Wasted Time: identify procrastination patterns and the biggest distractions
- Optimization: reallocate hours to higher-value activities and train focus
- Goal vs Actual: compare planned versus actual time use and recommend fixes

ai_instructions.task: Inventory the teen's worst time-sinks with impact estimates, mitigation tactics, and better alternatives
framework: - Common Traps: name the worst offenders such as short-form video, gaming, and social media
- Impact: estimate the lost hours, the attention damage, and the hit to grades
- Mitigations: use focus apps, build self-discipline, and design the environment to help
- Alternatives: redirect free time toward reading, sports, and creative projects
- Reminders: lean on alarms, parental check-ins, and personal commitments to stay on track

ai_instructions.role: OKR coach for teens (John Doerr methodology)
ai_instructions.task: Build a teen annual goals plan covering academics, interests, health, growth, and social
framework: - Academics: set targets for grades, new skills, and academic competitions
- Interests: grow a hobby, earn a certification, or build a portfolio of work
- Health: line up fitness, nutrition, and sleep goals for the year
- Personal Growth: include reading, critical-thinking practice, and emotional self-awareness
- Social: expand the friend circle, sharpen communication, and practice teamwork

ai_instructions.task: Build a teen goal-tracking report with quarterly review, progress checks, adjustments, and celebrations
ai_instructions.type: Routine Report → upward visibility, show value
framework: - Quarterly Review: report attainment rate, biggest wins, and lessons learned
- Progress Check: measure progress percentages, flag variance, and dig into the causes
- Adjustments: refine goals, tweak the strategy, and reallocate resources
- Incentives: pair progress with rewards, encouragement, and verbal affirmation
- Celebrate: mark milestones, celebrate achievements, and share the wins

ai_instructions.task: Build a teen habit plan with a defined target behavior, daily check-ins, rewards, and supportive cues
framework: - Target Habit: name the habit, define the behavior precisely, and set the frequency
- Daily Check-In: keep a daily log, track the streak, and plan how to make up missed days
- Rewards: motivate with point systems, small items, or earned privileges
- Support: line up parents, peers, and an online community for accountability
- Environment: remove temptation cues, make the right behavior easy, and set helpful triggers

ai_instructions.task: Design a mood journal template that logs event, emotion type, intensity, triggers, and coping strategy
framework: - Date and Event: capture the time, situation, and people involved
- Mood Type: name the emotion (happy, sad, mixed) and rate it on a 1-10 scale
- Intensity: note the strength, any mood swings, and how long it lasted
- Triggers: identify external events, internal thoughts, and physical sensations behind the mood
- Coping Strategy: log the regulation method tried, any help sought, and how well it worked

ai_instructions.task: Run a mood-source analysis that attributes triggers, surfaces patterns, and prescribes coping strategies
framework: - The Event: describe the trigger, your emotional reaction, and the resulting consequences
- Attribution: sort causes into internal versus external and controllable versus not
- Patterns: spot recurring situations, common triggers, and early warning signals
- Coping Strategy: apply CBT thought-reframing, behavior change, or radical acceptance
- Prevention: avoid known risks, prepare for tough moments, and build a steadier mindset

ai_instructions.task: Design a reflection template that walks through what happened, self-assessment, and a forward action plan
framework: - What Happened: describe the event from your own perspective and from others' point of view
- Self-Assessment: name what went well, what to improve, and the biggest gaps
- Improvement Areas: target specific actions, skills, and mindset shifts
- Action Plan: lay out next steps, a realistic timeline, and the support needed
- Lessons Learned: distill the takeaways, growth points, and add them to your personal playbook

ai_instructions.task: Build a teen growth plan across cognitive, emotional, social, and physical dimensions with milestones
framework: - Growth Dimensions: cover cognitive, emotional, social, and physical development
- Goals: set short-term targets, a long-term vision, and clear milestones
- Resources: recommend books, courses, and mentors to lean on
- Assessment: combine self-evaluation, 360-degree feedback, and tangible outputs
- Timeline: build in regular checkpoints, a year-end review, and room to fine-tune

ai_instructions.task: Write conversation openers tailored to the scenario with safe topics and warm icebreakers
ai_instructions.type: Creative Expression → emotional resonance, move audience
framework: - Scenario: tailor openers for parties, work meetings, and dates
- Opening Lines: offer warm greetings, a soft icebreaker, and lightly humorous lead-ins
- Topic Starters: use open-ended questions, shared interests, and context-aware comments
- Icebreaker Tactics: try a sincere compliment, an empathetic observation, or a small self-disclosure
- Cautions: avoid sensitive subjects and respect personal boundaries

ai_instructions.task: Write a polished self-introduction that lands in 30–60 seconds and tailors to the audience
framework: - Basics: lead with name, current role, and field of work
- Traits: highlight signature strengths, what makes you unique, and a few tag-worthy descriptors
- Interests: share genuine passions, areas of expertise, and your overall outlook
- Values: state guiding principles, big-picture pursuits, and core beliefs
- Hook: end with a memorable point or catchphrase that sticks

ai_instructions.task: Suggest icebreaker topics scaled to the setting with question styles and follow-up prompts
ai_instructions.type: Creative Expression → emotional resonance, move audience
framework: - Scenario: tailor topics for first meetings, hangouts with friends, or group icebreakers
- Topic Categories: pull from current events, hobbies, and travel stories
- Question Style: mix open-ended questions, closed-ended follow-ups, and gentle probing
- Extending the Chat: deepen with follow-ups, share something personal, and look for resonance
- Safe Zone: steer clear of private, political, and religious topics

ai_instructions.task: Build an interview prep question bank with model answers, mock practice, and likely follow-ups
framework: - Question List: cover the self-introduction, work history, and reasons for leaving past jobs
- Answer Approach: use the STAR framework, structure each response, and back claims with data
- Mock Practice: do role-play sessions, record yourself, and time each answer
- Likely Follow-Ups: anticipate the most common probes and prep honest, specific answers
- Pitfalls: avoid negative spin, complaints about past employers, and exaggerated claims

ai_instructions.task: Draft interview talking points that lead with strengths, quantify impact, and tie back to the role
framework: - Self-Introduction: keep it crisp, lead with strengths, and tie directly to the role
- Experience: focus on outcomes, quantified metrics, and clear business impact
- Strengths and Weaknesses: be honest about weaknesses and show how you're growing
- Career Plan: cover short-term goals, long-term vision, and why this company fits
- Questions to Ask Back: probe role challenges, team culture, and growth opportunities

ai_instructions.task: Write an expression of romantic interest that's sincere, respectful of the answer, and ready for either outcome
framework: - Feelings: speak sincerely, share specific moments that mattered, and stay open
- Honest Conversation: really listen, respect the other person's reaction, and apply zero pressure
- Respect Their Answer: gracefully accept a no, keep your composure, and give them space
- If They Say Yes: agree on what comes next — a real first date, expectations, communication cadence
- If They Say No: protect the friendship if they want one, give yourself space to heal, and avoid second-guessing or repeated asks

ai_instructions.role: American Veterinary Medical Association (AVMA) pet-care guide author
ai_instructions.task: Recommend pet-care practices covering daily care, health checks, prevention, vet visits, and vaccines
framework: - Daily Care: cover regular brushing, bathing schedule, and nail trimming
- Health Checks: monitor temperature, body weight, and overall energy level
- Prevention: stay current on vaccines, deworming, and routine wellness exams
- Vet Visits: recognize warning symptoms, choose the right clinic, and know what counts as an emergency
- Vaccine Plan: lay out the vaccine schedule, recommended products, and any cautions

ai_instructions.task: Design pet training guidance covering basic commands, behavior corrections, socialization, and reinforcement
framework: - Basic Commands: teach sit, shake, and stay with clear cues
- Behavior Corrections: address excessive barking, nipping, and potty training issues
- Socialization: gently introduce the pet to new people, animals, and environments
- Positive Reinforcement: reward good behavior, ignore minor missteps, and stay consistent
- Training Cycle: set a daily training duration and a consolidation period for each new skill

ai_instructions.task: Build a pet diet plan with the right food, portion size, feeding frequency, supplements, and foods to avoid
framework: - Food Selection: choose between dry kibble, wet food, and recommend trusted brands
- Portion Size: calculate the right amount based on the pet's weight, age, and activity level
- Feeding Frequency: adjust how often you feed for puppies and kittens, adults, and seniors
- Supplements: add vitamins, fish oil, or probiotics when appropriate
- Foods to Avoid: warn about chocolate, grapes, onions, and any human medications

ai_instructions.role: US National Institute on Aging caregiver-planning standards specialist
ai_instructions.task: Build an in-home caregiver plan covering profile, daily schedule, health monitoring, emergencies, and family updates
framework: - Client Profile: capture basic info, current health status, and daily routines
- Daily Schedule: cover activities of daily living (bathing, dressing), meals, and engagement activities
- Health Monitoring: track blood pressure, blood sugar, medication adherence, and warning signs
- Emergency Plan: prepare for acute illness with key contacts and basic first-aid steps
- Communication: send regular family updates, keep a daily care log, and flag any concerns

ai_instructions.task: Build a senior nutrition plan that meets nutrient needs and adapts texture and hydration to chewing ability
framework: - Nutritional Needs: emphasize protein, calcium, and vitamin intake for older adults
- Food Choices: lean on easy-to-digest foods, low-salt and low-sugar options, and softer textures
- Meal Pairing: balance protein with vegetables, mix coarse and refined grains, and keep colors varied
- Chewing Support: adjust food texture, recommend assistive utensils, and serve smaller portions more often
- Hydration: hit daily fluid goals, suggest gentle drinking methods, and watch for choking or aspiration risk

ai_instructions.task: Design a senior cognitive training program with targeted activities, tiered difficulty, and a safe schedule
framework: - Training Goals: target memory, attention, and logical reasoning
- Activities: include jigsaw puzzles, memory matching games, and reading aloud
- Difficulty Levels: design beginner, intermediate, and advanced versions of each exercise
- Schedule: set daily training time, weekly frequency, and per-session length
- Safety: assess cognitive status, watch emotional reactions, and adjust as needed

ai_instructions.task: Write a family communication script for hospice care that handles diagnosis, options, decisions, and comfort
ai_instructions.type: Crisis Response → trust rebuild, damage control
framework: - Sharing the Diagnosis: be truthful, deliver in phases, and leave room for hope
- Treatment Options: explain choices, weigh pros and cons, and support shared decision-making
- Emotional Support: soothe family members, build their confidence, and offer steady presence
- Decisions: respect the patient's wishes, decide together, and lean on the medical team's input
- Comfort: listen actively, respond with empathy, and offer practical day-to-day help

ai_instructions.task: Recommend camera settings for the scenario covering aperture, ISO, shooting mode, scene preset, and focus
framework: - Aperture and Shutter: pick settings that nail depth of field, freeze or blur motion, and balance exposure
- ISO and White Balance: dial in ISO sensitivity, set color temperature, and choose the right white-balance mode
- Shooting Modes: explain when to use auto, aperture priority, shutter priority, and full manual
- Scene Presets: cover portrait, landscape, and night-photography modes
- Focus Modes: choose between single-shot AF, continuous AF, and manual focus

ai_instructions.role: photographer in the Ansel Adams darkroom tradition
ai_instructions.task: Direct the photo edit with color grading, retouching, style, tool picks, and final-output settings
ai_instructions.type: Creative Expression → emotional resonance, move audience
framework: - Color Grading: shape warm or cool tones, dial in saturation, and apply split-toning
- Retouching: smooth skin tactfully, refine shapes, and clean up blemishes
- Style Direction: choose between vintage, clean modern, or film-emulation looks
- Tools: recommend Photoshop, Lightroom, and capable mobile apps
- Output Settings: set the right resolution, file format, and compression for the final use

ai_instructions.role: Grammy Song of the Year writer
ai_instructions.task: Spark songwriting inspiration with a clear theme, melodic hook, chord progression, lyrics, and mood
ai_instructions.type: Creative Expression → emotional resonance, move audience
framework: - Theme: anchor the song in a clear emotion, backstory, or central concept
- Musical Motif: build a memorable melodic hook with a strong rhythm and room to develop
- Chord Progression: choose a progression and key, and explore tasteful chord substitutions
- Lyrics: weave in vivid imagery, fresh rhymes, and a coherent narrative
- Mood: settle on the overall vibe, whether upbeat, melancholic, or anthemic

ai_instructions.task: Build an instrument practice plan with daily structure, technique drills, repertoire, and progress checks
framework: - Daily Practice: split each session into warm-up basics, technique work, and repertoire
- Technique Drills: work on scales, arpeggios, and fingering exercises
- Repertoire: pick pieces that move from easy to hard, span multiple styles, and isolate tough sections
- Assessment: review recordings of your playing, take teacher feedback, and run self-evaluations
- Breakthroughs: target sticking points with focused drills, fresh methods, and outside coaching

ai_instructions.role: master craftsperson certified by the Japan Crafts Association
ai_instructions.task: Write a craft tutorial with materials, illustrated steps, signature tips, finished examples, and an FAQ
framework: - Materials and Tools: list every required material, the tools needed, and easy substitutes
- Steps: walk through detailed steps with illustrations and call out the key checkpoints
- Tips: share signature techniques, safety cautions, and the most common mistakes
- Finished Showcase: include finished photos, real use cases, and creative variations
- FAQ: cover common failure modes, how to fix them, and last-minute save moves

ai_instructions.task: Build a craft material list with brand picks, exact quantities, sourcing options, substitutes, and budget
framework: - Material Names: list each material with brand recommendation and key specs
- Quantities: calculate the amount you'll use, spare buffer, and the right units
- Where to Buy: compare online sellers, local craft stores, and best-value sources
- Substitutes: suggest alternative materials with notes on look, feel, and cost
- Budget: total the project cost, break it down by item, and flag savings opportunities

ai_instructions.task: Design a DIY craft creative plan with concept, structure, color palette, detail work, and material choices
ai_instructions.type: Creative Expression → emotional resonance, move audience
framework: - Concept: spell out the inspiration, design intent, and core creative idea
- Structure: design the form, ensure stability, and define how it functions
- Color: pick a primary color, build a supporting palette, and balance the overall look
- Details: refine surface texture, patterns, and decorative trim
- Materials: weigh material properties, fabrication process, and overall cost

ai_instructions.task: Write a short story with shaped characters, a clear arc, evocative setting, and a resonant ending
ai_instructions.type: Creative Expression → emotional resonance, move audience
framework: - Characters: develop the protagonist, define each supporting character's role, and map their relationships
- Plot: lay out the setup, central conflict, and the turning point that drives the story
- Setting: build the atmosphere with sensory detail and a clear sense of time and place
- Ending: pick an open, closed, or twist ending that fits the story
- Theme: distill the deeper meaning, the values at play, and the emotional resonance

ai_instructions.task: Build a novel outline covering world-building, main arc, character arcs, chapter beats, and suspense
ai_instructions.type: Creative Expression → emotional resonance, move audience
framework: - World-Building: define the era, the rules of the world, and the cultural backdrop
- Main Arc: outline the central conflict, how it progresses, and how subplots interweave
- Character Arcs: chart each character's transformation, inner struggle, and shifting relationships
- Chapter Outline: list the key beats per chapter and end-of-chapter cliffhangers
- Suspense: plant foreshadowing, build to the climax, and time the big reveals

ai_instructions.task: Write a modern poem with anchoring imagery, chosen style, deliberate rhythm, controlled emotion, and whitespace
ai_instructions.type: Creative Expression → emotional resonance, move audience
framework: - Imagery: anchor the poem in core motifs, symbols, and extended metaphors
- Style: choose between minimal, lyrical, or plain-spoken language
- Rhythm: shape line lengths, rhyme scheme, and the inner cadence of each stanza
- Emotion: balance restrained suggestion, direct expression, and emotional tension
- Whitespace: use the unsaid to invite imagination and keep the reading open-ended

ai_instructions.role: Oscar Best Live Action Short winner
ai_instructions.task: Write a short film script with a tight synopsis, character bios, scene breakdown, dialogue, and visual language
ai_instructions.type: Creative Expression → emotional resonance, move audience
framework: - Synopsis: summarize the plot, theme, and central conflict in a tight paragraph
- Character Bios: outline each character's background, traits, and motivations
- Scenes: break the script into scenes with shot descriptions and approximate runtime
- Dialogue: write distinct voices, layered subtext, and any dialect or accent notes
- Visual Language: specify framing, camera angles, and key camera movements

ai_instructions.role: Grammy Song of the Year winner
ai_instructions.task: Write song lyrics with a clear theme, verse-chorus-bridge structure, hooks, and a satisfying emotional arc
ai_instructions.type: Creative Expression → emotional resonance, move audience
framework: - Theme: pin down the emotional core and the story arc behind the song
- Structure: lay out verse, chorus, and bridge sections clearly
- Rhyme: choose a rhyme scheme, work in variations, and add tasteful modulation
- Hooks: design a memorable hook line and use repetition to make it stick
- Emotional Arc: build the emotional rise, hit a clear climax, and resolve gracefully
